PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION DEMURRER FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITION IS UNTIMELY AS FILED OUTSIDE THE 90 DAY JURISDICTIONAL LIMITATION. PETITIONER'S RELIANCE ON GUEVARA FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT STATUTE WAS TOLLED IS MISPLACED. 28 USC 1367 IS NOT APPLICABLE. PETITIONER FAILS TO ALLEGE IN COMPLAINT OR STATE IN OPPOSITION THAT SHE CAN ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EQUITABLE TOLLING, SPECIFICALLY THAT RESPONDENT WAS A PARTY TO BOTH ACTIONS; PETITIONER HAD TIMELY NOTICE OF THE UD ACTION; AND THAT PETITIONER'S BELIEF THAT UD FILED BY A THIRD PARTY IS PROPER VEHICLE TO INITIATE JUDICIAL REVIEW OF RESPONDENT'S DECISION WAS REASONABLE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: THIS COURT ORDERS, ADJUDGES AND DECREES AS FOLLOWS: 1. PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS HEREBY DISMISSED AS AGAINST RESPONDENT THE RENT BOARD WITH PREJUDICE. 2. JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF RESPONDENT THE RENT BOARD, AND AGAINST PETITIONER. 3. THE RENT BOARD SHALL BE ENTITLED TO RECOVER AND IS HEREBY AWARDED ITS COSTS. (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Administrative Decision Pending The Court'S Decision On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Wolford, Esq., Attorney-At-Law, (415-265-2897), for Denise Beatty, Plaintiff and moving party.
Richard Simpson, Jr., Esq. of Wright Finlay & Zak, (949-477-5050), for U.S. Bank, Defendant and opposing party.
Wayne K. Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney, (415-554-4675), for SF Residential Rent Stabilization & Arbitration Board, Respondent.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER DENISE BEATTY's Motion For Stay Of Administrative Decision Pending The Court's Decision On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is DENIED. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Wolford, Esq., Attorney-At-Law, (415-265-2897), for Denise Beatty, Plaintiff and moving party.
Richard Simpson, Jr., Esq. of Wright Finlay & Zak, (949-477-5050), for U.S. Bank, Defendant and opposing party.
Wayne K. Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney, (415-554-4675), for SF Residential Rent Stabilization & Arbitration Board, Respondent.

ARGUED - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION is taken under submission. At 1:30pm, Court rules now adopting the tentative ruling as follows: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Petition is untimely as filed outside the 90 day jurisdictional limitation. Petitioner's reliance on Guevara for the proposition that statute was tolled is misplaced. 28 USC 1367 is not applicable. Petitioner fails to allege in complaint or state in opposition that she can allege facts in support of equitable tolling, specifically that respondent was a party to both actions; petitioner had timely notice of the UD action; and that petitioner's belief that UD filed by a third party is proper vehicle to initiate judicial review of respondent's decision was reasonable. Order is signed. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
